cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the question and the text carefully to determine the correct answer. The question asks which of the following descriptions about Yves Gomes is true. The answer choices are A, B, C, and D. Let me break them down one by one based on the information provided in the text. First, the passage tells us about Yves Gomes, a university student in Maryland who may have to leave the country. He is an illegal immigrant who grew up in the US and is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. His parents were deported more than a year ago. He wants to continue his education and eventually become a doctor. He supports the DREAMAct, which would provide a path to legal residency for undocumented immigrants who complete college or join the military. The DREAMAct has not passed in Congress, and Yves hopes it will pass soon so he can stay and become a US citizen. Now let's evaluate each option: (A) He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. The text says he was born in the US. It mentions he was brought to India by his parents when he was 14 months old, and then they were deported back to India more than a year ago. So he was born in the US, not India. So A is incorrect. (B) His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago. The text says "They were deported more than a year ago." "They" refers to his parents. The deportation would mean they were forced to leave the US, not India. The text doesn't say they were deported from India. It says Gomes was brought to India at 14 months old by his parents, implying they were in India. Then they were deported back to India, which suggests they were in the US and then sent back to their original country, India. So the statement that his parents were forced to leave India doesn't align with the text. Therefore, B is incorrect. (C) He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation. The text states he wants to become a doctor and stay in the US. It says he wants to finish school and eventually become a US citizen. He doesn't mention becoming a nurse in India. So C is incorrect. (D) He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct.
